Share sensitive information only on official, The UMLS integrates and distributes key terminology, classification and coding standards, and associated resources to promote creation of more effective and interoperable biomedical information systems and Sign up for a license, download files, and browse UMLS data The UMLS, or Unified Medical Language System, is a set of files and software that brings together many and biomedical vocabularies and standards to enable interoperability between computer systems.

UMLS licenses are issued only to individuals and not to groups or organizations. There is no charge for licensing the UMLS from NLM. NLM is a member of charge for SNOMED CT use in the United States and Some uses of the UMLS may require additional agreements with individual terminology vendors. The UTS account allows you to browse, download, and query the UMLS.

Link terms and codes between your doctor, your pharmacy, and your insurance company Coordinate patient care among several departments within a hospital Process texts to extract concepts, relationships, or knowledge Extract specific terminologies from the Metathesaurus Terms and codes from many vocabularies, including CPT, ICD-10-CM, LOINC, MeSH, RxNorm, and SNOMED definitions, and other relationships and attributes.

: Broad categories (semantic types) and their relationships (semantic : A large syntactic lexicon of biomedical and general English and tools for normalizing strings, generating lexical The UMLS Terminology Services (UTS) provides three ways to access the UMLS: You can search the data through UTS applications: - Retrieve UMLS concept information, including CUIs, semantic types, - View the names, definitions, and hierarchical structure of MetamorphoSys tool, included with the downloaded files, allows you to customize the UMLS according to your needs.

You can then load your customized data into your own database system, such as MySQL or Oracle, or you may browse your data using the MetamorphoSys RRF browser. If you use the UMLS data and tools in your work, please cite: UMLS Knowledge Sources [dataset on the Internet]. Release 2024AA.
